3A_Bibliographic.citation: He, X., Chen, Y., Zhang, W., & Li, C.-S. R. (2025). Elevated loss sensitivity in the reward circuit in adolescents with video game but not social media addiction. Computers in Human Behavior, 165, 108554.

3B_Sample.ABCD_release_version: unclear (curated dataset referenced, 2-year and 4-year follow-up data used)
3B_Sample.timepoints_used: Year 2 (2-year follow-up, ages 11-13) and Year 4 (4-year follow-up, ages 13-15)

3C_Exposure.exposure_measure: Video Game Addiction (VGA) Questionnaire and Social Media Addiction (SMA) Questionnaire
3C_Exposure.how_operationalized: 6-item questionnaires adapted from the Bergen Facebook Addiction Scale; Likert scale 1 (Never) to 6 (Very often); continuous scores used in linear mixed models

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Neural loss sensitivity (beta-weights for loss contrast in reward circuit ROIs during MID task)
3D_Outcome.measure_used: fMRI beta-weights for reward/loss contrasts from Monetary Incentive Delay (MID) task across 8 ROIs (nucleus accumbens, amygdala, caudate, putamen, insula, ACC, OFC, vmPFC)

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Model 1: Beta-weights ~ VGA + SMA + Time + VGA x Time + SMA x Time + Age + Sex + Race/Ethnicity + Family income + Mean framewise displacement + (1|Site). Model 2: VGA or SMA ~ Beta-weights + Time + Beta-weights*Time + Age + Sex + Race/Ethnicity + Family income + Mean framewise displacement + (1|Site). FDR-corrected with Benjamini-Hochberg.
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: Age, sex, race/ethnicity, family income, mean framewise displacement; research sites as random effects
3E_Design_Analysis.SES_in_model: yes - family income included as covariate in all models
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: We employed the linear mixed models (LMMs) in the R lme4 package to examine the interaction effects of VGA and SMA by time on neural responses to rewards and losses.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: VGA x Time interaction on loss contrast in left nucleus accumbens
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Significant interaction effects of VGA and time on neural responses to losses were observed in left nucleus accumbens (b = 0.106, SE = 0.034, 95% CI [0.040, 0.172], p = .002, pFDR = 0.032)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: VGA x Time interaction on loss contrast in left insula
VGA x Time interaction on loss contrast in right insula
VGA x Time interaction on loss contrast in right ACC
Neural loss sensitivity x Time interaction predicting VGA in right amygdala
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Significant interaction effects of neural loss sensitivity and time on VGA were observed in right amygdala (b = 0.086, SE = 0.029, 95% CI [0.029, 0.144], p = .003, pFDR = 0.048)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Neural loss sensitivity x Time interaction predicting VGA in right ACC
Change in VGA scores from Time 1 to Time 2
Change in SMA scores from Time 1 to Time 2

3G_Interpretation.verbatim_recommendation: Approaches such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, neuromodulation techniques, or digital interventions that promote healthier gaming habits might be informed by a deeper understanding of the specific neural circuits involved.

3H_Rhetorical.pattern_detail: Authors note 'the effect sizes observed in the current results are modest' and 'the overall levels of VGA and SMA in the sample are relatively low' but then state 'the large sample size and the longitudinal design have the potential to reveal significant impacts when considering the cumulative effects during adolescence' and proceed to recommend CBT and neuromodulation interventions.

3I_Flags.ambiguity_notes: Exposure is video game/social media addiction scores, not screen time per se. The fMRI outcome (neural loss sensitivity) is a neuroimaging marker, not a behavioral/mental health outcome. Small subsample (N=543) from the larger ABCD cohort. SMA showed no significant longitudinal effects.
